etymology.description: 進(進) (jìn, "to advance") and 入(入) (rù, "to enter") together convey the action of moving into a space, hence the meaning "enter" or "get into."
etymology.interpretation: The term 進入(進入) (jìnrù, "enter") combines 進(進) (jìn, "to advance") with 入(入) (rù, "to enter") to express the concept of advancing into or entering a space. This captures the seamless transition from outside to inside, whether a physical place or a metaphorical context.

examples.chinese: 她进入房间(她進入房間)。
太阳刚刚进入黄昏时(太陽剛剛進入黃昏時)，天空变得美丽无比(天空變得美麗無比)。
examples.pinyin: Tàiyáng gānggāng jìnrù huánghūn shí, tiānkōng biànde měilì wúbǐ.
examples.english: As the sun just entered the dusk, the sky became incredibly beautiful.

examples.chinese: 他进入了大学(他進入了大學)，开始他的学术旅程(開始他的學術旅程)。
examples.pinyin: Tā jìnrùle dàxué, kāishǐ tā de xuéshù lǚchéng.
examples.english: He entered the university, starting his academic journey.
